---
title: "Week 15 Guide"
subtitle: "Cumulative Application of Chapters 1–9"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

Week 15 is a cumulative application of everything from Chapters 1–9. You will work with a transaction log from a deep space trading post, applying the type conversion and DataFrame preparation skills from prior weeks alongside the visualization tools from Chapter 9. The assignment asks you to build a complete workflow from raw file to saved figure: load and prepare the data, answer a set of operational questions, produce four visualizations, and deliver a multi-panel summary figure to the waystation commander.

**Assignment:**

- Here is the link to the **[Week 15 Assignment](../../assignments/week-15/index.qmd)**.

## Concepts reinforced this week

This week applies the Chapter 9 visualization tools from the Week 14 demo: building bar charts, histograms, scatter plots, and box plots from a DataFrame using the Figure and Axes approach, and saving a figure to disk. Type conversion from Chapter 7 and DataFrame operations from Chapters 5 and 6 are part of the preparation workflow before any visualization can run.

## Week 15 tasks

1. Complete the **Week 15 Assignment** (Waystation Veridian trading post analysis).
2. Record and submit your video reflection.

## Knowledge goals for Week 15

By the end of Week 15, you should be able to:

* choose the appropriate visualization type for a given analytical question and explain why that type fits the question
* build bar, histogram, scatter, and box plots from a DataFrame using the Figure and Axes approach, directing each plot to a named Axes object
* interpret a scatter plot where no clear relationship is visible and explain what that result means analytically
* customize a figure with a descriptive title, labeled axes, and color-coded groups using Axes methods
* build a multi-panel figure and save it to a file at a specified resolution before displaying it
* explain why a figure must be saved before it is displayed and what happens if the order is reversed
